metadata.title: phone Sprint Zamanlaması ~4ms Doğruluğu Nasıl Sağlar
metadata.description: TrackSpeed'in phone kameranızı kullanarak milisaniye doğruluğunda sprint zamanlaması nasıl sağladığını öğrenin — ekstra ekipman gerekmez.

heroSubtitle: phone'un hassas sprint zamanlaması için ihtiyaç duyulan her şeye zaten sahip — yüksek kare hızlı kamera, hassas jiroskop ve güçlü işlemci. Bunun neler sağladığı aşağıda.

subFrame.title: Kare Altı Hassasiyet
subFrame.desc: Sporcular bir kare yakalandığında tam kapıyı nadiren geçer. Sistemimiz, kareler arasındaki geçiş anını tespit etmek için birden fazla kareyi analiz eder — kameranın yerel kare aralığının çok ötesine geçen milisaniye altı interpolasyon doğruluğu sağlar.
subFrame.detail: The interpolation works by observing the athlete's torso position in at least 3 consecutive frames as they approach and cross the timing line. By fitting a linear motion model to these positions, we solve for the precise timestamp when the torso center crossed the gate position. Because human sprinting velocity is nearly constant over short distances (the 1-2 meter detection zone), the linear model produces highly accurate results.

corrections.title: Fizik Tabanlı Düzeltmeler
corrections.desc: phone kameraları her kareyi birkaç milisaniye boyunca yukarıdan aşağıya tarar; bu, sporcunun karede göründüğü yere bağlı olarak zamanlama kaymasına yol açar. Sistematik hatayı ortadan kaldırmak için bunu ve diğer donanım düzeyindeki zamanlama yapıtlarını otomatik olarak düzeltiriz.
corrections.detail: TrackSpeed automatically measures the rolling shutter duration for each device and applies a per-pixel timing correction. If an athlete's torso is detected at 40% of the frame height and the rolling shutter scan takes 10ms, we apply a 4ms correction to the timestamp. This correction is applied before interpolation, ensuring the trajectory regression operates on accurate timing data. The result: rolling shutter becomes a non-factor in the final timing.

clockSync.title: Çoklu Cihaz Saat Senkronizasyonu
clockSync.desc: Biri başlangıçta biri bitişte olmak üzere iki phone kullanırken saatlerinin milisaniyeler içinde uyuşması gerekir. Senkronizasyon protokolümüz cihazlar arasında ±3-5ms hizalama sağlar. Senkronizasyon sırasında en iyi sonuçlar için her iki telefonu birbirine yaklaşık 10 metre mesafede tutun. Senkronize olduktan sonra istediğiniz kadar uzaklaştırabilirsiniz.
clockSync.detail: The sync protocol sends multiple round-trip timing probes between the two phones via peer-to-peer WiFi. By measuring send and receive timestamps on both devices, we calculate the clock offset using the same mathematical principles as the Network Time Protocol (NTP). Multiple samples are taken and outliers are rejected, achieving ±3-5ms alignment between devices. During sync, keep both phones within about 10 meters of each other for best results. Once synced, move them as far apart as needed — the offset stays locked.

bodyTracking.title: Vücut Takibi, Işın Kırma Değil
bodyTracking.desc: Lazer kapılar ışını ilk kıran şey üzerinde tetiklenir — uzatılmış bir kol, diz veya göğüs. Bu, koşu formu nedeniyle değişir ve tutarsızlık yaratır. TrackSpeed, gövdenin ne zaman çizgiyi geçtiğini belirlemek için sporcunun vücut kütlesini takip eder; bu da resmi zamanlamanın çalışma şekliyle örtüşür.
bodyTracking.detail: TrackSpeed's detection engine replicates the official approach. Our geometry-based algorithm processes each video frame at 180×320 resolution, using frame differencing to detect motion, connected component labeling to identify body regions, and size/shape filters to isolate the torso from limbs. An arm-spike suppression algorithm prevents raised arms from triggering false crossings. The result: consistent chest-based timing that matches the official standard, unlike laser gates that trigger on the nearest body part.

deepDive.title: The Detection Pipeline
deepDive.intro: Every video frame captured by your phone's camera goes through a multi-stage detection pipeline. Here's what happens in the roughly 2ms it takes to process each frame:

downsample.title: 1. Downsample & Extract Luma
downsample.desc: The raw camera frame is downsampled to 180×320 pixels and converted to a grayscale (luma) image. This resolution is sufficient for accurate body detection while keeping processing fast enough for real-time analysis at 120fps.

frameDiff.title: 2. Frame Differencing
frameDiff.desc: Each frame is compared against the previous frame (N-1 differencing). Pixels that changed significantly — above a fixed threshold of 15 — indicate motion. This produces a binary motion mask showing where the athlete is moving.

ccl.title: 3. Connected Component Labeling
ccl.desc: The motion mask is analyzed using an 8-connected component labeling algorithm, which groups adjacent motion pixels into distinct blobs. Each blob is a candidate body region.

filters.title: 4. Size & Shape Filters
filters.desc: Candidate blobs are filtered by physical plausibility. A valid body region must be at least 33% of the frame height and 8% of the frame width. Fill ratio (minimum 0.20) and aspect ratio (maximum 1.2) filters reject noise, shadows, and camera shake artifacts.

crossing.title: 5. Gate Crossing Detection
crossing.desc: When a validated body region crosses the timing line position, a crossing event is triggered. A 0.5-second cooldown prevents double-counting from the same athlete, and gate-occupied tracking ensures each crossing is recorded exactly once.

interpolation.title: 6. Position-Based Interpolation
interpolation.desc: The crossing timestamp is refined using trajectory regression across the surrounding frames. This is where the sub-frame precision comes from — converting a discrete frame event into a continuous-time measurement with ~4ms accuracy.

stability.title: Device Stability & Thermal Management
stability.desc: Accurate detection requires a stable camera. TrackSpeed monitors your phone's gyroscope in real-time, alerting you if the device moves during timing. The IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit) detects rotation rates as small as 0.15 rad/s — about the level of vibration from a passing truck.
stability.thermal: Running the camera at 120fps is computationally intensive. TrackSpeed continuously monitors the device's thermal state and will warn you if the phone is overheating, which can cause frame drops and reduce timing accuracy. For sessions longer than 30 minutes, we recommend using 60fps, which maintains ~6ms accuracy with significantly lower thermal impact.

accuracy.title: Sonuç
accuracy.label: Etkin zamanlama doğruluğu
accuracy.desc: Kare altı interpolasyon, fizik düzeltmeleri ve saat senkronizasyonu bir araya gelerek — yalnızca phone'unuzu kullanarak — profesyonel donanımla rekabet eden zamanlama hassasiyeti sunar.

items.question: How accurate is phone-based sprint timing compared to laser gates?
items.answer: TrackSpeed achieves ~4ms effective timing accuracy, which is more consistent than laser gates for training purposes. Laser gates have sub-millisecond trigger speed, but they trigger on whichever body part breaks the beam first — hand, knee, or chest — creating 50-200ms of run-to-run variability. TrackSpeed tracks the chest crossing, matching official timing standards and eliminating this inconsistency.

items.question: Does TrackSpeed work without internet or WiFi?
items.answer: Yes. Single-phone timing works completely offline. Multi-phone timing uses peer-to-peer WiFi Direct between the devices — no internet connection or router needed. The phones connect directly to each other.

items.question: Can TrackSpeed replace professional timing equipment?
items.answer: For training purposes, yes. TrackSpeed provides the consistency and accuracy needed to track progress, compare training sessions, and make coaching decisions. For official competition results that go on record, Fully Automatic Timing (FAT) systems with photo finish cameras remain the standard — they achieve 0.001s resolution. TrackSpeed is designed for the 99% of timing that happens in training, not the 1% that happens at meets.

items.question: Why does frame rate matter for timing accuracy?
items.answer: Higher frame rates give more data points for trajectory regression. At 30fps, frames are 33.3ms apart; at 60fps, 16.7ms; at 120fps, 8.3ms. More frames near the crossing moment means better interpolation accuracy. However, even at 30fps, sub-frame interpolation achieves ~10ms accuracy — still far better than manual stopwatch timing (~200ms human reaction time error).

items.question: How does multi-phone timing work?
items.answer: Place one phone at the start line and one at the finish. The phones connect via peer-to-peer WiFi and synchronize their clocks using an NTP-style protocol (±3-5ms accuracy). Each phone independently detects when the athlete crosses its line. The finish phone subtracts the start timestamp from the finish timestamp to calculate the elapsed time, corrected for the measured clock offset.

items.question: What happens if my phone overheats during a session?
items.answer: TrackSpeed monitors thermal state and warns you before accuracy degrades. At 120fps, sustained recording can cause thermal throttling after 20-30 minutes, leading to dropped frames. If this happens, the app suggests switching to 60fps, which uses significantly less power while maintaining ~6ms timing accuracy. For long practice sessions, 60fps is recommended from the start.

professionalComparison.title: Karşılaştırma
professionalComparison.laserNote: *Lazer kapılar milisaniye altı hızda tetiklenir; ancak tutarsız tetikleme noktaları (kol - göğüs) aynı sporcu için koşular arasında 50-200ms varyasyona neden olabilir.

cta.title: Antrenmanını zamanlamaya hazır mısın?
cta.subtitle: TrackSpeed'i indir ve yalnızca phone'unla milisaniye hassasiyetini deneyimle.
cta.compare: TrackSpeed'in diğer zamanlama yöntemleriyle karşılaştırmasına bak
